free_text: Activation cross section measurement of deuteron
induced reactions on nat-Ni with spetial reference
to monitoring beam performances and production of
Cu-61 medical isotope
Main reference.
Reference containing revised
data for 61Cu.
, cyclotron lab in Debrecen
, cyclotron lab in Brussels
10 micron Ni foils from goodfellow
5 mm collimated beam on target
the bombarding beam intensity was monitored continously
during the irradiation and was kept constant within 5%
charge was collected in Faraday-cup and was monitored
with 27-Al(d,x)24-Na monitor reaction. The flux value
based on the monitor reaction was 10% higher than the
direct beam current measurement.
in the calculation of cross section data the
charge integrated by faraday-cup was used. Nuclear
data were taken from E.Browne and R.B.Firestone, Table
of radioactive isotopes, ed.V.S.Shirley, Wiley 1986.
targets were irradiated for 1. hour, with 200 Na
deuteron beam with 10., 16.7 And 20.3 MeV
primary energies.
the error of the energy was calculated taking
into account the initial uncertainty (+-0.3 MeV in
brussel and +- 0.2 MeV in Debrecen), the straggling
effect of the beam and the uncertainty in the thickness
of the foils.
the total energy error varied from 0.2 MeV to 1.0 MeV.
the error of the cross section data were
estimated by summing up the squares of the individual
relative errors and taking the square root of it.
the typical error was around 12%.
O.Schwerer, K.Okomot
status report on cross section of monitor reactions for
radioisotope production, IAEA-report INDC(NDS)-218/GZ,
Vienna, Austria,1989.
data were taken from the table 3.of the manuscr.
approved by S. Takacs. 29/11/1996
BIB section upgrated
KB New reference and new subentry D4045009
were added which contain revised data for 61Cu.
Subentry D4045008 was superseded.

These results were obtained by the authors after a
strict revision of data of the main reference and were
reported in NIMB 174 (2001) 235.
